diagnoses.name: Acute embolism and thrombosis of left iliac vein
diagnoses.evidence_span: Imaging studies revealed acute embolism and thrombosis of the left iliac vein

diagnoses.name: Compression of left iliac vein
diagnoses.evidence_span: acute embolism and thrombosis of the left iliac vein with associated vein compression

diagnoses.name: Iatrogenic vascular injury during circulatory system procedure
diagnoses.evidence_span: an accidental puncture and laceration of a circulatory system organ occurred

diagnoses.name: Complication of surgical procedure
diagnoses.evidence_span: during the initial catheter-based procedure, an accidental puncture and laceration of a circulatory system organ occurred

procedures.name: Percutaneous dilation of left common iliac vein with intraluminal device placement
procedures.evidence_span: Initial therapy included dilation of the left common iliac vein with placement of an intraluminal device via percutaneous approach

procedures.name: Percutaneous introduction of thrombolytic agent via peripheral vein
procedures.evidence_span: Thrombolytic therapy was introduced via peripheral vein access

procedures.name: Percutaneous extirpation of thrombus from left femoral vein
procedures.evidence_span: extirpation of thrombus was performed from both the left femoral vein and left common iliac vein

procedures.name: Percutaneous insertion of intraluminal device into inferior vena cava
procedures.evidence_span: An intraluminal device was placed into the inferior vena cava percutaneously

procedures.name: Percutaneous extirpation of thrombus from left common iliac vein
procedures.evidence_span: extirpation of thrombus was performed from both the left femoral vein and left common iliac vein (procedure repeated to ensure complete clearance)

procedures.name: Percutaneous dilation of left external iliac vein with intraluminal device placement
procedures.evidence_span: the left external iliac vein was dilated with placement of another intraluminal device

medications.name: Heparin sodium
medications.evidence_span: Anticoagulation was initiated with intravenous heparin sodium

medications.name: Alteplase
medications.evidence_span: intravenous heparin sodium and alteplase infusions, with doses titrated based on coagulation studies

medications.name: Enoxaparin sodium
medications.evidence_span: The patient also received enoxaparin sodium subcutaneously

medications.name: Warfarin
medications.evidence_span: transitioned to warfarin with apixaban for long-term anticoagulation coverage

medications.name: Apixaban
medications.evidence_span: transitioned to warfarin with apixaban for long-term anticoagulation coverage

medications.name: Oxycodone immediate release
medications.frequency: every 4–6 hours as needed for pain
medications.evidence_span: Pain control was achieved with oxycodone immediate release, hydromorphone, and fentanyl infusions

medications.name: Hydromorphone
medications.evidence_span: Pain control was achieved with oxycodone immediate release, hydromorphone, and fentanyl infusions

medications.name: Fentanyl
medications.evidence_span: Pain control was achieved with oxycodone immediate release, hydromorphone, and fentanyl infusions

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.evidence_span: with acetaminophen used as adjunctive therapy

medications.name: Clonazepam
medications.evidence_span: Clonazepam was administered for anxiety management

medications.name: Levothyroxine sodium
medications.evidence_span: The patient received levothyroxine sodium for thyroid replacement at 75 mcg daily

medications.name: Ranitidine
medications.evidence_span: Gastrointestinal protection was maintained with ranitidine

medications.name: Polyethylene glycol
medications.evidence_span: bowel function was supported with polyethylene glycol and docusate sodium

medications.name: Docusate sodium
medications.evidence_span: bowel function was supported with polyethylene glycol and docusate sodium

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.evidence_span: Electrolyte repletion included potassium chloride (both oral and intravenous)

medications.name: Magnesium sulfate
medications.evidence_span: Electrolyte repletion included potassium chloride (both oral and intravenous) and magnesium sulfate

medications.name: Phenazopyridine
medications.evidence_span: Phenazopyridine was given for dysuria symptoms

medications.name: Duloxetine
medications.evidence_span: Duloxetine was continued at 120 mg daily for depression management

chief_complaint: Left lower extremity swelling and pain
